mentions.note: Earnings Thursday night; part of mega-cap tech cluster that has coincided with market peaks and subsequent selloffs.
Part of data center boom; Cramer wishes trust owned more; beneficiary of compute shortage.
Mega-cap tech earnings tomorrow (earnings D-Day); key risk if big capex without matching revenue growth. Held in trust.
Parabolic move threatening to touch sun; correction needed. Owned in trust but stock has run too far; pullback coming.
Down 4% YTD, trades 12× earnings despite excellent management; undervalued opportunity in well-run bank.
Speculative biotech heavily shorted with no earnings; only buy if you believe in life-saving potential. Otherwise, move on.
War in Iran cutting into world travel demand; company reporting weakness; risk spreads to hotels, resorts, cruise lines, airlines.
Real uranium company with actual production, not mock uranium ETF; legitimate winner.

Parabolic move; had Icarus moment down 9% today despite announcing two huge clients. Stock needed the rain; sell sell sell.

Good stock in bad oil markets, great in good ones; trading inexpensively with strong upside.
Technology terrific but earnings blah; non-growth stock disqualifies from recommendation on growth-focused show.
Spin-off story: June 30 split into industrial automation and aircraft businesses. Aircraft will be fantastic once war ends; stock undervalued during breakup purgatory.
Well down from its highs with great balance sheet and diversified pharma/medical device portfolio; preferred healthcare play.

Caller bought at 85, sold half at 150 for profit; holding remainder with house money. Let it run; consider taking profit at 200.

At 16-year high, raised guidance, technology finally proven; long-term winner after years of struggle.

Wall Street Journal article raised concerns but likely overblown; CFO Sarah Fryer is trustworthy. Not a shorting opportunity but caution warranted on spend-without-revenue risk.

No growth; stock flat since 2022 and even after recent upgrade. Cramer only buys growth stocks.
